facts: The Big Bang created primarily Hydrogen (75%) and Helium (25%) in the early universe.
Stars are cosmic furnaces, fusing these lighter elements into heavier ones like Carbon, Oxygen, and Iron.
Massive stars end their lives in supernovae, violently scattering these newly forged 'stellar seeds' across galaxies.
Our solar system, Earth, and all life formed from clouds enriched by these ancient stellar explosions.
idea.payoff: Every atom in your body (except most of the hydrogen) was forged inside an ancient star!
idea.title: You're Stardust! ✨🤯

scenes.visual: A swirling, mysterious cosmic cloud, dark with hints of light.
Depiction of the early universe expanding, with simple H and He atoms floating.

scenes.text: Stars fuse H & He into Carbon, Oxygen, Iron.
scenes.visual: A close-up of a vibrant, hot star, showing simplified fusion reactions within its core.

scenes.text: Supernovae scatter these 'stellar seeds' across space.
scenes.visual: A spectacular supernova explosion, with bright elements radiating outwards into a dark cosmos.

scenes.text: You are Stardust. Every atom forged in a star!
scenes.visual: Transition from a colorful nebula to a silhouette of Earth, then zooming into a human silhouette filled with glowing 'stardust' particles.
